rationale: Both papers are centrally about structural modeling of TV viewing behavior with heterogeneity and switching/state-dependence dynamics: the cited paper builds a viewing choice model with latent segments and heterogeneous switching costs, while the citing paper claims to extend TV viewing/ad-avoidance modeling via a continuous-time household-level framework. Given this strong methodological/domain overlap, the citation is plausibly foundational or method-relevant rather than an obvious incidental mention, so it should be retained for full relation judgment.
